tagline: Calculate depth of field, hyperfocal distance, and near/far focus limits for any lens
keywords: depth of field, DOF calculator, hyperfocal distance, bokeh, sharp zone

faq.question: What is depth of field?
faq.answer: Depth of field (DOF) is the range of distances in a photo that appear acceptably sharp. A shallow DOF (like f/1.4) blurs backgrounds for portraits. A deep DOF (like f/11) keeps everything sharp for landscapes.

faq.question: What affects depth of field the most?
faq.answer: Aperture has the biggest effect (wider = shallower DOF). Focus distance is next (closer = shallower). Focal length and sensor size also matter. Portraits at f/1.4 on a 85mm lens give very shallow DOF.

faq.question: What is hyperfocal distance?
faq.answer: The hyperfocal distance is the focus point that maximizes DOF from half that distance to infinity. It's critical for landscape photography. At f/11 on a 24mm full-frame lens, the hyperfocal distance is about 5.5 feet.

formulas: Hyperfocal Distance H = f² / (N × CoC)
Near Limit = (H × D) / (H + (D - f))
Far Limit = (H × D) / (H - (D - f))
DOF = Far Limit - Near Limit

tagline: Estimate customs duties for common product categories imported to the US
keywords: customs duty, HTS code, import duty rate, customs tariff, product tariff

faq.question: How do I find the exact duty rate for my product?
faq.answer: Look up your product's HTS (Harmonized Tariff Schedule) code at hts.usitc.gov. Each product has a specific 10-digit code with an exact duty rate. A customs broker can classify your goods.

faq.question: What products have the highest import duties?
faq.answer: Clothing/textiles (up to 32%), footwear (up to 48%), dairy products (up to 25%), and tobacco products face the highest US import duties. Electronics and machinery often enter at 0-5%.

faq.question: Do I pay duty on personal purchases shipped from abroad?
faq.answer: US residents get an $800 duty-free exemption per person for goods purchased abroad. Above $800, you'll pay duty on the excess amount. De minimis threshold for imports is $800 (raised from $200 in 2016).

formulas: Base Duty = Declared Value × Duty Rate%
Additional Tariff = Declared Value × Additional Rate%
Total Duty = Base Duty + Additional Tariffs + Fees
Actual rates vary by specific HTS classification

tagline: Calculate quarterly estimated taxes for gig workers, freelancers, and 1099 contractors
keywords: gig worker tax, 1099 tax, quarterly taxes, self employment tax, freelance tax

faq.question: When are quarterly estimated taxes due?
faq.answer: Q1: April 15, Q2: June 15, Q3: September 15, Q4: January 15 (next year). If the due date falls on a weekend/holiday, the deadline moves to the next business day.

faq.question: How much should gig workers set aside for taxes?
faq.answer: A safe rule is 25-30% of net income. This covers both self-employment tax (15.3%) and federal income tax (10-24% for most gig workers). State taxes may add another 3-10%.

faq.question: What is the 2024 standard mileage rate?
faq.answer: The IRS standard mileage rate for 2024 is 67 cents per mile for business use. Track all business miles — for gig workers (Uber, DoorDash, etc.), this is often the largest deduction.

formulas: Net Income = Gross - Expenses - Mileage Deduction
SE Deduction = SE Tax × 50% (deductible on income tax)
Quarterly Payment = SE Tax + Estimated Income Tax

tagline: Calculate your life path number, destiny number, and personal year from your birth date
keywords: numerology calculator, life path number, destiny number, personal year, numerology meaning

faq.question: What is a life path number?
faq.answer: Your life path number is calculated by reducing your full birth date to a single digit (or master number 11, 22, 33). It's considered the most important number in numerology, revealing your life purpose and key traits.

faq.question: What are master numbers in numerology?
faq.answer: Master numbers (11, 22, 33) are not reduced to single digits. They carry heightened energy and potential. 11 is the Intuitive, 22 is the Master Builder, and 33 is the Master Teacher.

faq.question: How do personal year cycles work?
faq.answer: Personal years run in 9-year cycles. Calculate by reducing your birth month + birth day + current year. Each year has a theme: Year 1 is new beginnings, Year 9 is completions and endings.

formulas: Life Path = Reduce(Month) + Reduce(Day) + Reduce(Year) → reduce to single digit
Personal Year = Reduce(Birth Month + Birth Day + Current Year)
Reduce: sum digits repeatedly until single digit (or master number)
Master Numbers (11, 22, 33) are not reduced further

tagline: Discover your aura color based on personality traits and energy assessment
keywords: aura color, aura reading, energy color, chakra color, spiritual assessment

faq.question: What is an aura?
faq.answer: In spiritual traditions, an aura is an energy field surrounding the body. Different colors are believed to reflect personality, emotional state, and spiritual development. While not scientifically validated, aura reading is a popular self-reflection tool.

faq.question: What does each aura color mean?
faq.answer: Red: passionate, strong, grounded. Orange: creative, adventurous. Yellow: optimistic, intellectual. Green: healing, nurturing. Blue: calm, communicative. Indigo: intuitive, wise. Violet: spiritual, visionary. White: pure, transcendent.

faq.question: Can your aura color change?
faq.answer: Yes. Aura colors are believed to shift based on emotional state, health, spiritual growth, and life circumstances. Your core aura color tends to remain stable, but secondary colors fluctuate regularly.

formulas: Primary color mapped from dominant personality trait
Secondary color complements the primary energy
Chakra alignment follows the traditional 7-chakra system
Stress level affects aura clarity and vibrancy

tagline: Calculate the ideal carbon-to-nitrogen ratio for your compost pile using common materials
keywords: compost ratio, carbon nitrogen ratio, composting calculator, C:N ratio, compost mix

faq.question: What is the ideal compost ratio?
faq.answer: The ideal carbon-to-nitrogen (C:N) ratio for composting is 25:1 to 35:1, with 30:1 being optimal. This provides the right balance for microorganisms to decompose materials efficiently.

faq.question: What are browns and greens in composting?
faq.answer: Browns are carbon-rich materials: dry leaves, straw, cardboard, wood chips. Greens are nitrogen-rich: grass clippings, food scraps, coffee grounds, manure. You need roughly 3 parts brown to 1 part green by volume.

faq.question: Why does my compost smell bad?
faq.answer: A smelly compost pile usually has too much nitrogen (green material). Add more browns (dry leaves, cardboard), turn the pile for aeration, and ensure it's not too wet. A healthy pile should smell earthy.

formulas: Weighted C:N = (Brown lbs × Brown C:N + Green lbs × Green C:N) ÷ Total lbs
Ideal ratio: 25:1 to 35:1 (target 30:1)
General rule: 3 parts brown to 1 part green by volume
Composting time decreases with optimal ratio, moisture, and aeration
